missing_perspectives.who: Ibu Soko and female customary leaders (Adat Perpatih)
missing_perspectives.what: Ibu Soko Klana Hulu and Anak Waris Klana Hulu refused to give Mubarak a mandate. Matrilineal structure means women hold authority in lineage, inheritance, and customary legitimacy.
missing_perspectives.why: Rejection of Mubarak's declaration came not only from male officeholders but from female customary leadership whose consent is essential under Adat Perpatih.

missing_perspectives.who: The other three Undang Yang Empat (Jelebu, Johol, Rembau)
missing_perspectives.what: Whether the other three support or oppose Mubarak's declaration determines whether this is isolated or a broader customary challenge. 'Three of the four Undangs' were reportedly involved (per The Edge).
missing_perspectives.why: Critical to assessing the constitutional implications if a majority of the electoral body were to act.

alternative_framings.current: Dispute as question of legal authority — whether dismissed Undang retains power to depose Ruler.
alternative_framings.alternative: Dispute as crisis of Adat Perpatih's democratic and consensual character. Mubarak's unilateral declaration violates core procedural norm of consensus (muafakat), regardless of formal legal standing.
alternative_framings.evidence: Telaga Undang official Sarifah Norzaidah Al-Qadri stated Mubarak acted 'without any customary or constitutional mandate'; Ibu Soko and Anak Waris Klana Hulu 'never gave him a mandate'.

alternative_framings.current: 2008 proclamation as ironic fact undermining Mubarak's credibility.
alternative_framings.alternative: 2008 election was itself a departure from 1967 precedent when Tuanku Muhriz (then 19) was bypassed for his uncle Tuanku Ja'afar. Suggests Undang's electoral discretion is substantive — this dispute may continue unresolved tensions from 1967-2008 succession.
alternative_framings.evidence: The Edge: in 1967 Tuanku Muhriz was 'deemed too young for the throne as he was 19'. Upon Ja'afar's 2008 death, Undang 'revisited that earlier decision'.

international_parallels.rule_or_statistic: College of Chiefs may designate a successor; may declare king ought to cease to hold office on grounds of infirmity or misconduct, subject to joint Parliament sitting.
international_parallels.lesson: Modern constitutions can codify both customary electoral role of chiefs and procedurally-constrained circumstances under which monarch may be removed.

international_parallels.rule_or_statistic: Druk Gyalpo may be removed by joint Parliament sitting with ≥2/3 present and voting, on grounds of intentional constitutional violation or permanent mental disability.
international_parallels.lesson: A codified removal process can exist — but requires explicit constitutional provision, not unilateral customary assertion.

counter_arguments.counter: If Undang Yang Empat collectively possess power to elect YDPB, arguable under customary constitutional principles they retain residual power to remove in extraordinary circumstances. Articles 10-12 may be silent on removal rather than prohibitive of it.

Mubarak's 2025 dismissal itself may be contested on procedural grounds. Exact nature of 33 offences not publicly disclosed. If dismissal is invalid under adat, subsequent declaration — while legally ineffective under state law — might carry customary weight among adherents.

missing_facts.fact: Telaga Undang official Sarifah Norzaidah Al-Qadri: 'Ibu Soko Klana Hulu and Anak Waris Klana Hulu also never gave him a mandate'; matter referred to NS Islamic Religious Affairs Department and police.

Mubarak's dismissal announced by Tua Lembaga Waris di-Darat Datuk Andulika Mandalika Zainol Ariffin Ibrahim after consent from Ibu Soko Klana Hulu and Anak Waris Klana Hulu, meeting at Telaga Undang Sungai Ujong on 1 April 2025.
14th Undang Luak Johol Datuk Mohammad Jan Abdul Ghani dismissed 27 March 2016 for 13 offences (precedent).
DKU functions under Article 16 of UUTKNS 1959.
Tuanku Muhriz invoked Clause 24 UUTKNS 1959 during 17 April 2026 DKU sitting to invite Ibu Soko Klana Hulu and Waris Klana Hulu.
1967: Tuanku Muhriz (then 19) bypassed; Tuanku Ja'afar elected; reigned until 2008.
Undang of Sungai Ujong chosen among Waris Klana di-Hulu and di-Hilir families; inherits Dato' Klana Petra title; succession matrilineal and elective per Adat Purbakala.
Telaga Undang: traditional meeting place in Luak Sungei Ujong, Kampung Telaga Undang, Seremban; rotates between four designated family houses.

gender_gaps.missing: Role of women in Adat Perpatih — particularly Ibu Soko whose consent is required for customary decisions including Mubarak's dismissal and any mandate for action against the Ruler.
How the dispute might differently affect women — rural women dependent on adat-governed land; women in political leadership navigating modern governance and customary expectations.

economic_gaps.missing: Potential effect on investor confidence, state government continuity, and RM8 billion investment inflow target for NS 2026.
Economic interests tied to customary land (tanah adat) governed by Adat Perpatih.

geographic_gaps.missing: Centered on Seremban, Seri Menanti, administrative/royal centres. No examination of dispute perception in rural luak territories where Adat Perpatih is lived practice.
East Malaysia — Sabah/Sarawak native customary law systems offer comparative insights.

overall_assessment: Stage 1 strong on legal/constitutional framing. Narrowly focused on apex institutional actors. Omits: women customary leaders (Ibu Soko), other three Undang Yang Empat, grassroots adat practitioners, B40 communities, youth, Orang Asli. Lacks economic analysis; does not explore gender dimensions of matrilineal system in crisis. Should incorporate: 1967 succession bypass context, procedural details of Mubarak's dismissal (Ibu Soko role), comparative lessons from Lesotho/Bhutan.
